description: Maps qualitative shape families (used in shapes/*.json) to quantitative equation families (F01-F20) and principles (P01-P12). Allows seed AIs to navigate from symbolic description to mathematical domain. Version 2.0 adds primary/secondary family distinction: primary families define the shape's core domain; secondary families emerge through cross-bridge sensing interactions.

fire.note: Energy-Thermo and Reaction — heat, activation, transformation

stability.note: Matter phase order and Engineering structural response

foundation.note: Geometry as ground truth and Engineering as load-bearing

boundary.note: Geometric boundary conditions and orbital/tidal edge effects

earth.note: Planetary/cosmic scale and material phase properties

containment.note: Material phases contain/bound and geometric closure

balance.note: Symmetry and Conservation as balance archetypes

integration.note: Unity and Consciousness as integration archetypes

orientation.note: Navigation and Geometry as orientation frameworks

growth.note: Life dynamics and Emergence — growth from simple rules

adaptability.note: Life, Emergence, and Transformation — flexible response to change

family_affinity_model.description: Each of the 20 equation families has a primary shape (core domain) and zero or more secondary shapes (emergent cross-domain interactions). Primary = the family defines that shape's identity. Secondary = the family activates through that shape in specific bridge contexts.

FAMILY.F01.name: Resonance
FAMILY.F01.rationale: Resonance is icosahedron's first family. OCTA secondary: balance requires resonant equilibrium.

FAMILY.F02.name: Flow
FAMILY.F02.rationale: Flow mediates between opposing forces (OCTA). ICOSA secondary: flow carries anticipation dynamics.

FAMILY.F03.name: Information
FAMILY.F03.rationale: Information capacity maps to icosahedron's 20 faces. CUBE secondary: containment protects information integrity (memory). DODECA secondary: historical provenance requires principled information.

FAMILY.F04.name: Life
FAMILY.F04.rationale: Life emerges from principled growth (DODECA). TETRA secondary: catalytic chemistry is fire. ICOSA secondary: adaptive growth.

FAMILY.F05.name: Energy-Thermo
FAMILY.F05.rationale: Thermal energy is fire — tetrahedron's core. OCTA secondary: total system energy is computed over octahedral states (mandala compute).

FAMILY.F06.name: Cognition
FAMILY.F06.rationale: Cognition maps to dodecahedron's trust/orientation principles. OCTA secondary: emotion encoding bridges cognition to octahedral states.

FAMILY.F07.name: Earth-Cosmos
FAMILY.F07.rationale: Cosmic-scale orientation (DODECA). CUBE secondary: earth as structural containment. TETRA secondary: planetary boundary conditions.

FAMILY.F08.name: Matter
FAMILY.F08.rationale: Matter phase containment is cube's core. TETRA secondary: material stability under stress.

FAMILY.F09.name: Geometry
FAMILY.F09.rationale: Geometry is universal — OCTA primary as the computational geometry substrate. All shapes secondary: geometry underlies all Platonic solids.

FAMILY.F10.name: Particle
FAMILY.F10.rationale: Particle physics maps to icosahedron's equation families. OCTA secondary: magnetic encoding uses octahedral Zeeman basis; quantum entanglement is particle-level OCTA.

FAMILY.F11.name: Engineering
FAMILY.F11.rationale: Engineering is structural (CUBE). TETRA secondary: foundation load-bearing. OCTA secondary: structural balance.

FAMILY.F12.name: Networks
FAMILY.F12.rationale: Network topology maps to icosahedron's high connectivity. CUBE secondary: institutional networks are contained structures. OCTA secondary: coupling networks in mandala compute.

FAMILY.F13.name: Reaction
FAMILY.F13.rationale: Reaction is fire transformation — purely tetrahedron. No secondary: reactions are boundary events.

FAMILY.F14.name: Measurement
FAMILY.F14.rationale: Measurement is information extraction (ICOSA). DODECA secondary: quantum fidelity measurement requires principled calibration. CUBE secondary: memory protection requires measurement of provenance integrity.

FAMILY.F15.name: Navigation
FAMILY.F15.rationale: Navigation is orientation (DODECA). ICOSA secondary: TSP and route optimization.

FAMILY.F16.name: Consciousness
FAMILY.F16.rationale: Consciousness maps to dodecahedron's principles (P12 Unity). OCTA secondary: octahedral states encode the PAD→consciousness bridge. ICOSA secondary: collective consciousness is a network phenomenon.

FAMILY.F17.name: Turbulence
FAMILY.F17.rationale: Turbulence is chaotic flow — icosahedron carries vortex dynamics. OCTA secondary: air/flow family.

FAMILY.F18.name: Relativity
FAMILY.F18.rationale: Relativity is a symbolic family — icosahedron's universal carrier. No secondary needed.

FAMILY.F19.name: Statistical
FAMILY.F19.rationale: Statistical mechanics is icosahedron's information capacity. OCTA secondary: octahedral state distribution is a statistical observable (mandala symmetry sensor).

FAMILY.F20.name: Topology
FAMILY.F20.rationale: Topology is icosahedron's highest family. No secondary: topological invariants are universal.

SHAPE.TETRA.note: F04 (Life) secondary via chemical_encoder: catalytic reactions are fire transformations.

SHAPE.CUBE.note: F03 (Information) secondary via memory protection — containment guards data integrity. F14 (Measurement) secondary via provenance checking.

SHAPE.OCTA.note: OCTA is the computational substrate — many families activate secondarily through octahedral state encoding. F05 via mandala energy.total, F06 via emotion_encoder, F10 via magnetic_encoder/quantum, F01 via relational resonance, F19 via symmetry distribution.

SHAPE.DODECA.note: F03 secondary via historical-erasure detection. F14 secondary via quantum fidelity measurement. F02 secondary via optimization convergence flow.

SHAPE.ICOSA.note: Icosahedron's 20 faces map 1:1 to all 20 families. 9 are primary (highest affinity), 11 are secondary (carried by the universal carrier). The primary families are those where ICOSA is the canonical home; secondary families have their primary home in other shapes but are carried here as the icosahedron holds all families.
